name: engineering-quality-pass-20260404-0444
idea: Engineering quality pass: selection + context menu + shot creation system. Review and simplify all code across the recent selection/context-menu/shot-creation features for maximum simplicity and clean abstractions. Areas to cover: (1) GallerySelectionContext — is the API surface minimal? Is registerPeerClear the right abstraction? (2) useSelectedMediaClips + gallery selection merging in AgentChat — can this be simpler? (3) SelectionContextMenu — is it clean, minimal, well-abstracted? (4) Shot creation path (createShotWithGenerationsPath, shotCreationAction changes) — does it fit cleanly into the existing shot creation system? (5) GenerationsPaneGallery — it now handles selection, lasso, context menu, shot creation, video modal — is it getting too big? Should anything be extracted? (6) ClipAction.tsx — are the new items cleanly integrated? (7) useExternalDrop multi-drag — is it clean? (8) useTimelineState peer clear wiring — is it the right level of abstraction? (9) useLassoSelection, useModifierKeys — clean and minimal? (10) dragDrop.ts multi-generation type — clean? Priority: simplicity, nice abstractions, clear responsibility boundaries. Don't over-abstract but don't leave things tangled either.

history.message: execute output missing required keys: output, files_changed, commands_run, deviations

overrides.note: User confirms: Q1 keep serial insertion with a comment explaining why (RPC auto-calculates position from max frame). Q2 existing integration coverage is sufficient, no new test file needed. Q3 two-panel selection is the stable architecture, registerPeerClear stays as-is.
